properties.name: Strait of Hormuz
properties.current_threat: IRGC naval harassment, Houthi missile threat, mine-laying operations, tanker seizures
properties.description: The world's most critical oil chokepoint. 21 million barrels per day flow through this 34km-wide passage between Iran and Oman. Closure would trigger immediate global energy shock. Iran has repeatedly threatened to close the strait as leverage against Western sanctions.

properties.name: Bab al-Mandab Strait
properties.current_threat: Active Houthi drone, missile and naval drone attacks since November 2023. Major shipping rerouting via Cape of Good Hope.
properties.description: Gateway between the Red Sea and Gulf of Aden. Houthi attacks since 2023 have forced 60%+ of container shipping to divert 6,000nm around Africa, adding $1M+ per voyage. Nine major submarine cables transit here.

properties.name: Strait of Malacca
properties.current_threat: PLAN military exercises, piracy, territorial disputes between Singapore/Malaysia/Indonesia, cyber attacks on port systems
properties.description: The world's busiest shipping lane. 80,000+ vessels annually carrying 25% of global trade including 15 million barrels of oil per day. Only 2.8km wide at its narrowest.

properties.name: Suez Canal
properties.current_threat: Houthi-induced traffic diversion, Egyptian political instability, Israeli conflict spillover, accident vulnerability
properties.description: 12-15% of global trade including 30% of global container traffic transits this 193km canal. The 2021 Ever Given grounding blocked $9.6B of trade per day for 6 days. Egypt earns $8B/year in tolls.

properties.name: Panama Canal
properties.current_threat: Severe drought reducing water levels (2023 record lows forced 40% traffic reduction), Chinese port investment at both ends via Panama Ports Company
properties.description: 3-5% of global trade including critical US-Asia container routes. 2023 drought forced canal to operate at 60% capacity. Chinese companies control port operations at both Atlantic and Pacific entrances.

properties.name: Taiwan Strait
properties.current_threat: PLAN military exercises simulating blockade (2022, 2023), missile overflights, air incursion into ADIZ, economic coercion against Taiwan
properties.description: 50% of global container shipping passes through or near this 180km strait. TSMC produces 90% of world's most advanced chips on Taiwan. A PLAN blockade would simultaneously sever semiconductor supply chains and disrupt global shipping.

properties.name: South China Sea
properties.current_threat: Chinese artificial island militarization, PLAN harassment of Philippines/Vietnam fishing vessels, 9-dash line enforcement, US FONOP responses
properties.description: $3.4 trillion in trade annually. China claims 90% via nine-dash line, rejected by international tribunal. Spratly and Paracel Islands militarized with runways, missile systems, and naval facilities.

properties.name: Bosphorus / Dardanelles
properties.current_threat: Turkey using Montreux Convention to block Russian warships, Russian Black Sea Fleet pressure, grain/oil export leverage, Turkish strategic ambiguity
properties.description: Only exit for Black Sea nations. Russian grain and oil exports depend on this passage. Turkey invoked Montreux Convention in 2022 to block Russian warships. 800M+ people's food security depends on Ukrainian/Russian grain exports through this passage.

properties.name: GIUK Gap
properties.current_threat: Russian submarine patrols testing NATO acoustic surveillance, Northern Fleet deployment exercises, Svalbard archipelago disputes
properties.description: Greenland-Iceland-UK gap is NATO's critical anti-submarine barrier. Russia must pass through to reach Atlantic. SOSUS hydrophone arrays monitor passage. In a NATO-Russia conflict, Russian submarines would attempt to breach this gap to interdict trans-Atlantic supply convoys.

properties.name: Cape of Good Hope
properties.current_threat: Surging traffic from Red Sea diversion causing port congestion at Cape Town/Durban, limited infrastructure, South African political instability
properties.description: Emerged as critical alternative following Houthi Red Sea attacks. Traffic increased 40%+ since late 2023. Route adds 6,000nm and 10-14 days to Europe-Asia voyages. South African port infrastructure not designed for this volume.

properties.name: Lombok Strait
properties.current_threat: PLAN submarine transit to avoid Malacca shallow waters, Indonesian maritime sovereignty concerns, limited surveillance
properties.description: Deep water alternative to Malacca Strait allowing PLAN nuclear submarines to transit between Indian and Pacific Oceans undetected. 40m minimum depth vs Malacca's 23m. Preferred route for Chinese Type 094 ballistic missile submarines.

properties.name: Mozambique Channel
properties.current_threat: Mozambique LNG insurgency disrupting Chinese BRI port investments, Islamist insurgency in Cabo Delgado, Chinese naval access to Nacala and Pemba ports
properties.description: 600km channel between Madagascar and Mozambique. Strategic for Cape diversion shipping and Chinese BRI port strategy. China invested $5B in Mozambique. Islamist insurgency threatens LNG operations and port infrastructure.

properties.name: Luzon Strait
properties.current_threat: PLAN exercises in Philippine Sea, Chinese pressure on Philippine fishing vessels at Scarborough Shoal, US-Philippines treaty obligations tension
properties.description: 350km strait between Taiwan and Luzon (Philippines) is PLAN's primary deep-water access from South China Sea to Pacific Ocean. Critical submarine route to PLAN's Pacific deterrent patrol areas. Eight submarine cables transit here.

properties.name: Siliguri Corridor (Chicken's Neck)
properties.current_threat: Chinese infrastructure development along Bhutan border narrowing corridor, PLAN positioning in Chumbi Valley, Bangladesh instability, India-China border tensions since 2020 Galwan clash
properties.description: 22km-wide land corridor connecting mainland India to its entire northeastern states (population 45 million). China controls terrain on three sides. Severance would instantly isolate Assam, Arunachal Pradesh, Nagaland, Manipur. India's greatest geographic vulnerability.

properties.name: Suwalki Gap
properties.current_threat: Russian-Belarusian military exercises near gap, Wagner Group Belarus presence, Kaliningrad missile deployment, Russian hybrid operations in Lithuania/Poland
properties.vulnerability_type: NATO Land Corridor Severance / Baltic Encirclement
properties.description: 65km land corridor between Poland and Lithuania — the only overland connection between NATO's Baltic states and the rest of NATO. On one side is Russian Kaliningrad (nuclear weapons, Iskander missiles), on the other Belarus. NATO's most identified conventional warfare vulnerability in Europe.

properties.name: Arctic Northwest Passage
properties.current_threat: Russia claiming Arctic sovereignty, Canada-US disputes over territorial waters, Russian military Arctic buildup, climate opening accelerating strategic competition
properties.description: Climate change is opening Arctic shipping routes 2-3 months annually, reducing Asia-Europe distances by 40% versus Suez. Russia operates Northern Sea Route and claims exclusive control. Projected to be commercially viable year-round by 2035-2040.

properties.name: Kola Peninsula Approaches
properties.current_threat: Russia Northern Fleet nuclear submarine base at Severomorsk, SSBN patrol routes to Atlantic, NATO maritime patrol aircraft surveillance, Norwegian monitoring networks
properties.description: The Kola Peninsula hosts 60%+ of Russia's nuclear submarine fleet. These submarines must transit to the Atlantic and Arctic to maintain deterrent patrols. NATO anti-submarine warfare assets position to monitor these transit routes.

properties.name: Baltic Sea Exits (Denmark Straits)
properties.current_threat: Nord Stream pipeline sabotage precedent, Baltic cable cutting incidents (2023-2024), Russian Baltic Fleet operational pressure, Sweden/Finland NATO membership escalation
properties.description: Denmark Straits (Great Belt, Little Belt, Oresund) are sole exits for Russia's Baltic Fleet. Nord Stream sabotage (2022) demonstrated vulnerability. Multiple Baltic cable cutting incidents attributed to Russian shadow fleet in 2023-2024.

properties.name: Gulf of Guinea
properties.current_threat: Russian Wagner/Africa Corps influence in surrounding states (Mali, Niger, Burkina Faso, CAR), maritime piracy resurgence, Chinese port investments, West African coups destabilizing maritime security
properties.description: Gulf of Guinea produces 4-5 million barrels/day. Wagner/Africa Corps coup wave in Sahel is encircling coastal West African states. Piracy attacks surged. Chinese port investments at Lome, Tema, Cotonou create dual-use access potential.

properties.name: Hormuz-Malacca Energy Corridor
properties.current_threat: Indian Ocean power competition: US 5th Fleet, Indian Navy, PLAN expansion, submarine operations, anti-ship missile proliferation across multiple nations
properties.description: The entire Indian Ocean arc from Hormuz through Bab al-Mandab, around Sri Lanka, and through Malacca constitutes the single largest energy corridor on Earth. 40% of all global oil transits this route. Simultaneous disruption at two or more chokepoints would trigger a cascading global energy crisis.
